info.name: OpenAI API
info.description:
# OpenAI API — Postman Collection

A hand-curated collection covering the most-used REST endpoints of the [OpenAI Platform API](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference). Every request below has a long-form description explaining each body/query parameter, plus a short pre-request script with `//` comments (since JSON request bodies cannot contain inline comments — Postman would send them to the API verbatim and the API would reject them).

## Authentication

Authentication is configured **once** at the collection level (Bearer token) and inherited by every request. Open the collection's **Authorization** tab to inspect/override it. The token comes from the `OPENAI_API_KEY` environment variable.

Optional headers (set on individual requests when needed):

| Header | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `OpenAI-Organization` | Bill usage to a specific org (defaults to your default org). |
| `OpenAI-Project` | Scope the request to a project under the org. |
| `OpenAI-Beta` | Required for beta endpoints (e.g. `assistants=v2`). |

## Environment variables

Import the companion environment file (`openai-api.postman_environment.json`) and set these:

| Variable | Required | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `OPENAI_API_KEY` | ✅ | — | Secret API key from <https://platform.openai.com/api-keys>. Stored as a **secret** type variable. |
| `BASE_URL` | ✅ | `https://api.openai.com/v1` | Override only if you're proxying or hitting a compatible gateway. |
| `DEFAULT_CHAT_MODEL` | ⬜ | `gpt-4o-mini` | Used by chat/responses requests. Pick whatever you have access to. |
| `DEFAULT_EMBEDDING_MODEL` | ⬜ | `text-embedding-3-small` | Used by `/embeddings`. |
| `DEFAULT_IMAGE_MODEL` | ⬜ | `gpt-image-1` | Used by `/images/*`. |
| `DEFAULT_TTS_MODEL` | ⬜ | `gpt-4o-mini-tts` | Used by `/audio/speech`. |
| `DEFAULT_STT_MODEL` | ⬜ | `gpt-4o-mini-transcribe` | Used by `/audio/transcriptions` and `/audio/translations`. |
| `OPENAI_ORG_ID` | ⬜ | — | Sent as `OpenAI-Organization` header when set (handled by the collection-level pre-request script). |
| `OPENAI_PROJECT_ID` | ⬜ | — | Sent as `OpenAI-Project` header when set (same script). |

## Quick start

1. **Import** this file and `openai-api.postman_environment.json` into Postman.
2. Select the `OpenAI` environment from the env switcher (top-right).
3. Paste your key into `OPENAI_API_KEY` (the **current value** column, not the initial value — keeps the secret off your sync).
4. Open **Chat Completions → Create chat completion** and hit **Send**.

## Folders

- **Models** — list / retrieve available models.
- **Chat Completions** — the workhorse `/chat/completions` endpoint with variants: streaming, tool calling, vision, structured outputs.
- **Responses API** — the newer `/responses` endpoint (stateful, multimodal, tools first-class).
- **Embeddings** — vector embeddings for retrieval / similarity.
- **Images** — generate, edit, and vary images.
- **Audio** — speech synthesis, transcription, translation.
- **Files** — upload assets used by fine-tuning, batch, assistants, vision, etc.
- **Moderations** — content safety classifier.
- **Fine-tuning** — create and manage fine-tuning jobs.
- **Batches** — async bulk processing at 50% cost.

## Streaming requests

Requests with `"stream": true` (e.g. *Streaming chat completion*) return Server-Sent Events. Postman renders the stream in the response viewer line-by-line — leave the request open while events arrive.

## References

- API reference: <https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference>
- Model catalog: <https://platform.openai.com/docs/models>
- Pricing: <https://openai.com/api/pricing/>
- Status: <https://status.openai.com>

exec: // Collection-level pre-request script.
// Runs before EVERY request in the collection.
// Purpose: conditionally attach org / project scoping headers when
// the corresponding environment variables are set, so individual
// requests don't have to repeat this boilerplate.
const orgId = pm.environment.get('OPENAI_ORG_ID');
const projectId = pm.environment.get('OPENAI_PROJECT_ID');
if (orgId && orgId.trim() !== '') {
pm.request.headers.upsert({ key: 'OpenAI-Organization', value: orgId });
if (projectId && projectId.trim() !== '') {
pm.request.headers.upsert({ key: 'OpenAI-Project', value: projectId });
// Sanity-check the API key is set; emit a console warning rather than
// hard-failing so people can still inspect the prepared request.
if (!pm.environment.get('OPENAI_API_KEY')) {
console.warn('[OpenAI collection] OPENAI_API_KEY is not set in the active environment.');
// Collection-level test script. Runs after every response.
// Adds a couple of universally-useful assertions and surfaces
// OpenAI's standard error envelope when present.
pm.test('Response is JSON or a stream', () => {
const ct = pm.response.headers.get('Content-Type') || '';
pm.expect(ct).to.match(/json|event-stream|audio|image|octet-stream|text/i);
if ((pm.response.headers.get('Content-Type') || '').includes('application/json')) {
try {
const body = pm.response.json();
if (body && body.error) {
console.error('[OpenAI error]', body.error);
} catch (e) {
// Non-JSON or empty body — fine, skip.

variable.description: Fallback base URL. Overridden by the environment's BASE_URL if defined.

item.name: Models
item.description: Discover which models your key can call and inspect their metadata.

request.description:
## `GET /v1/models`

Returns every model the authenticated key has access to, including their IDs, owners, and creation timestamps.

### Use this when
- Verifying which fine-tuned models are visible.
- Building a model picker UI.
- Sanity-checking that a freshly-rotated key works.

### Response shape
```json
{
  "object": "list",
  "data": [
    { "id": "gpt-4o-mini", "object": "model", "created": 1715367049, "owned_by": "system" }
  ]
}
```

[Docs](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/models/list)

variable.description: Model ID, e.g. `gpt-4o-mini`, `text-embedding-3-small`, or a fine-tune like `ft:gpt-4o-mini:acme::abc123`.

request.description:
## `GET /v1/models/{model}`

Fetch metadata for a single model by ID. Useful when you want to confirm ownership/creation time of a fine-tune or surface model details in tooling.

[Docs](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/models/retrieve)

item.name: Chat Completions
item.description: The `/chat/completions` endpoint: stateless, single-turn-in/single-turn-out. The de-facto OpenAI workhorse — most variations (streaming, tools, vision, structured outputs) are flavors of the same request shape.

exec: // Body anatomy:
//   model       — required; which model to invoke.
//   messages    — required; ordered conversation history. Each
//                 entry has a role ('system' | 'user' | 'assistant'
//                 | 'tool') and content (string or content-parts).
//   temperature — 0..2; lower = more deterministic.
//   max_tokens  — caps the *completion* length, not total tokens.
//                 (Use `max_completion_tokens` on reasoning models.)
//   top_p       — nucleus sampling; usually leave at 1.
//   n           — how many completions to return.
//   stop        — string or array; halts generation when seen.
//   user        — opaque end-user identifier for abuse monitoring.

request.description:
## `POST /v1/chat/completions`

The classic chat endpoint. Send a list of messages, get a single assistant message back.

### Body parameters
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `model` | string | ✅ | Model ID, e.g. `gpt-4o-mini`. |
| `messages` | array | ✅ | Conversation history. Roles: `system`, `user`, `assistant`, `tool`. |
| `temperature` | number | ⬜ | Sampling temperature `[0, 2]`. Default `1`. Lower → deterministic. |
| `top_p` | number | ⬜ | Nucleus sampling. Use `temperature` *or* `top_p`, not both. |
| `n` | int | ⬜ | How many completions to generate. Default `1`. |
| `max_tokens` | int | ⬜ | Hard cap on completion tokens. (Reasoning models use `max_completion_tokens`.) |
| `stop` | string \| array | ⬜ | Stop sequences. |
| `presence_penalty`, `frequency_penalty` | number | ⬜ | `[-2, 2]`. |
| `logit_bias` | object | ⬜ | Token-id → bias `[-100, 100]`. |
| `response_format` | object | ⬜ | `{ "type": "json_object" }` or `{ "type": "json_schema", ... }`. See *Structured outputs*. |
| `tools`, `tool_choice` | — | ⬜ | See *Tool calling (function calling)* variant. |
| `stream` | bool | ⬜ | If `true`, returns SSE. See *Streaming chat completion*. |
| `seed` | int | ⬜ | Best-effort determinism. |
| `user` | string | ⬜ | Opaque end-user ID. |

### Response
```json
{
  "id": "chatcmpl-...",
  "object": "chat.completion",
  "created": 1730000000,
  "model": "gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18",
  "choices": [
    {
      "index": 0,
      "message": { "role": "assistant", "content": "..." },
      "finish_reason": "stop"
    }
  ],
  "usage": { "prompt_tokens": 23, "completion_tokens": 18, "total_tokens": 41 }
}
```

[Docs](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/create)

exec: // With stream=true the response is a Server-Sent Events stream:
//   data: {"id":"...","choices":[{"delta":{"content":"Hello"}}]}
//   data: {"id":"...","choices":[{"delta":{"content":" world"}}]}
//   data: [DONE]
// Each chunk's `delta` carries the *incremental* content to append.
// `stream_options.include_usage`: ask the final chunk to include
// token usage stats (useful for cost accounting).

request.description:
## Streaming variant

Sets `stream: true` and asks for usage stats in the final chunk. Postman renders SSE line-by-line — keep the response panel open while events arrive.

The terminating chunk is the literal line `data: [DONE]`.

[Streaming docs](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/streaming)

exec: // Tool calling (a.k.a. function calling) lets the model emit a
// structured request to invoke one of *your* functions.
// Round-trip:
//   1. You send `tools` (JSON-schema function defs) + a user msg.
//   2. Model responds with assistant message whose `tool_calls`
//      array names a function and supplies JSON arguments.
//   3. You execute that function locally.
//   4. You append the result as a { role: 'tool', tool_call_id, content }
//      message and call again. Model produces the user-facing reply.
// `tool_choice`:
//   'auto'  — model decides (default when tools are present)
//   'none'  — never call a tool
//   'required' — must call at least one
//   { type:'function', function:{ name:'...' } } — force a specific call

request.description:
## Tool calling (function calling)

The model returns a `tool_calls` array instead of (or alongside) a content string. Your app executes the call and continues the conversation with a `role: "tool"` message containing the result.

See `tool_choice` options in the pre-request script.

[Tool calling docs](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/function-calling)

## Vision input

When `content` is an array of *content parts*, you can mix text and images. Images can be remote URLs **or** base64 data URLs (`data:image/png;base64,...`).

`image_url.detail`:
- `"low"`  — single 512×512 tile (cheap).
- `"high"` — multi-tile, higher token cost.
- `"auto"` — model decides (default).

Requires a vision-capable model (e.g. `gpt-4o`, `gpt-4o-mini`, `gpt-4-turbo`).

[Vision docs](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/vision)

item.name: Chat completion with structured outputs (JSON schema)

exec: // `response_format.type = "json_schema"` constrains the output to
// match a JSON Schema *exactly* (no escapes, no hallucinated fields).
// `strict: true` enables 100% schema adherence — required fields,
// no additional properties, enum-only values, etc.
// For looser "please return JSON" use `response_format: { type: 'json_object' }`.

request.description:
## Structured outputs

Forces the assistant message to be valid JSON matching the supplied schema. With `strict: true`, the model is guaranteed (subject to OpenAI's enforcement) to never emit fields you didn't declare.

[Structured outputs docs](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/structured-outputs)

item.name: Responses API
item.description: The newer `/responses` endpoint. Stateful (server-side conversation state via `previous_response_id`), multimodal, and treats tools (function/web/file/computer use) as first-class. Recommended over `/chat/completions` for new builds.

body.raw:
{
  "model": "{{DEFAULT_CHAT_MODEL}}",
  "input": "Write a haiku about API testing.",
  "instructions": "You are a terse poet. Output only the haiku, no commentary.",
  "temperature": 0.8,
  "max_output_tokens": 200,
  "store": true
}

request.description:
## `POST /v1/responses`

### Body parameters
| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `model` | string | Required. |
| `input` | string \| array | Required. A bare string is shorthand for `[{ role: 'user', content: [...]}]`. Use array form for multi-part input (text + image + file). |
| `instructions` | string | System-style prompt; replaces the system message. |
| `previous_response_id` | string | Continue a stored prior response — the server replays its history. |
| `tools` | array | Mix of `{type:'function',...}`, `{type:'web_search_preview'}`, `{type:'file_search'}`, `{type:'computer_use'}`, etc. |
| `tool_choice` | string \| object | Same semantics as chat. |
| `temperature`, `top_p`, `max_output_tokens` | numbers | As you'd expect. |
| `store` | bool | Persist the response server-side so you can chain via `previous_response_id`. |
| `stream` | bool | Emits typed SSE events (`response.created`, `response.output_text.delta`, `response.completed`, …). |
| `metadata` | object | Up to 16 key/value pairs you can later filter on. |

### Response
Contains `output[]` (array of typed items: messages, tool calls, refusals) and convenience accessor `output_text`.

[Responses docs](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/responses)

variable.description: ID returned by *Create response* (only if you set `store: true`).

request.description:
Fetch a stored response by ID. Useful for resuming a multi-turn flow from a different client.

[Docs](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/responses/get)

Permanently delete a stored response and its associated state.

[Docs](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/responses/delete)

item.name: Embeddings
item.description: Turn text into vectors for retrieval, clustering, or semantic search.

exec: // `input` can be:
//   - a string                (1 embedding back)
//   - an array of strings     (N embeddings back, batched)
//   - an array of token-id arrays (rare; precomputed tokenization)
// `dimensions` only applies to `text-embedding-3-*` models — you can
// trim to fewer dims (e.g. 256, 512, 1024) to save vector-DB storage.
// `encoding_format`: 'float' (default) or 'base64' for compact transport.

item.name: Images
item.description: Generate, edit, and create variations of images.

body.raw:
{
  "model": "{{DEFAULT_IMAGE_MODEL}}",
  "prompt": "A friendly cartoon postman robot delivering API calls, flat vector art",
  "n": 1,
  "size": "1024x1024",
  "quality": "high",
  "output_format": "png"
}

request.description:
## `POST /v1/images/generations`

### Body parameters
| Field | Description |
|---|---|
| `model` | `gpt-image-1` (recommended) or legacy `dall-e-3` / `dall-e-2`. |
| `prompt` | Free-text description. `gpt-image-1` accepts up to 32k chars. |
| `n` | How many images. |
| `size` | `1024x1024`, `1024x1536`, `1536x1024`, `auto`. (DALL·E 3 sizes differ.) |
| `quality` | `low` / `medium` / `high` / `auto`. Cost scales with quality. |
| `output_format` | `png` (default), `jpeg`, `webp` (gpt-image-1 only). |
| `background` | `transparent` / `opaque` / `auto`. Transparent requires PNG/WebP. |
| `response_format` | `url` (DALL·E only) or `b64_json`. `gpt-image-1` always returns base64. |

[Docs](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/images/create)

formdata.description: PNG/JPEG/WebP, up to 25 MB. Multiple `image` fields accepted (gpt-image-1).
Optional. Same size as `image`. Transparent pixels = areas to edit.

formdata.value: Replace the background with a sunset over mountains.

request.description:
## `POST /v1/images/edits`

Multipart form. Provide `image` (and optionally `mask`) plus a `prompt` describing the edit.

In Postman, click the **Select Files** button on each `image` / `mask` row to attach.

[Docs](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/images/createEdit)

formdata.description: Variations are only supported by `dall-e-2` at the moment.

request.description:
## `POST /v1/images/variations`

Produce a riff on a source image without a text prompt. DALL·E 2 only.

[Docs](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/images/createVariation)

## `POST /v1/audio/speech`

Returns raw audio bytes (not JSON). In Postman, click **Save Response → Save to a file** to listen.

### Body parameters
| Field | Description |
|---|---|
| `model` | `gpt-4o-mini-tts`, `tts-1`, or `tts-1-hd`. |
| `input` | Up to 4096 chars. |
| `voice` | `alloy`, `ash`, `ballad`, `coral`, `echo`, `fable`, `onyx`, `nova`, `sage`, `shimmer`, `verse`. |
| `response_format` | `mp3` (default), `opus`, `aac`, `flac`, `wav`, `pcm`. |
| `speed` | `0.25`–`4.0`. |
| `instructions` | (gpt-4o-mini-tts only) free-form delivery direction, e.g. "calm, professional". |

[Docs](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/audio/createSpeech)

formdata.description: Audio file: mp3, mp4, mpeg, mpga, m4a, wav, webm. Max 25 MB.
Optional. Style/vocabulary hint; must match transcript language.

request.description:
## `POST /v1/audio/transcriptions`

Speech → text in the original language.

[Docs](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/audio/createTranscription)

## `POST /v1/audio/translations`

Non-English speech → English text.

[Docs](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/audio/createTranslation)

item.name: Files
item.description: Upload and manage files used by fine-tuning, batch, assistants, vision, etc.

query.description: Cursor: file ID to paginate after.

request.description:
List uploaded files visible to the active org/project.

[Docs](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/files/list)

formdata.description: How OpenAI will use this file. Drives validation: `fine-tune` requires JSONL with the right shape; `batch` requires the batch JSONL format; `vision` accepts images; `assistants` is general-purpose; `user_data` is generic; `evals` for the evals API.

request.description:
## `POST /v1/files`

Max file size: 512 MB. Org has a 100 GB total cap.

[Docs](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/files/create)

Get metadata for one file (status, bytes, purpose, created_at).

[Docs](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/files/retrieve)

Download the raw bytes of a stored file.

[Docs](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/files/retrieve-contents)

Permanently delete a file.

[Docs](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/files/delete)

item.name: Moderations
item.description: Classify whether content violates OpenAI's usage policies.

request.description:
## `POST /v1/moderations`

Free to call. `omni-moderation-latest` is multimodal (text + image). Returns per-category booleans and scores.

[Docs](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/moderations)

item.name: Fine-tuning
item.description: Create and manage fine-tuning jobs that turn a base model into a domain-specialized model.

request.description:
## `POST /v1/fine_tuning/jobs`

`training_file` must be a previously-uploaded file with `purpose="fine-tune"` (JSONL of chat-shaped examples).

Set `suffix` to give the resulting model a memorable name: `ft:gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18:org::my-experiment:abc123`.

[Docs](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/fine-tuning/create)

List jobs visible to the active org/project.

[Docs](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/fine-tuning/list)

Get status (`validating_files`, `queued`, `running`, `succeeded`, `failed`, `cancelled`) and final model ID if done.

[Docs](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/fine-tuning/retrieve)

Stream of human-readable status events for a job (validation, epoch progress, etc.).

[Docs](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/fine-tuning/list-events)

Cancel a queued or running job. No body required.

[Docs](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/fine-tuning/cancel)

item.name: Batches
item.description: Submit a JSONL of API requests to be processed asynchronously within 24h at 50% cost. Great for offline embeddings, scoring, etc.

request.description:
## `POST /v1/batches`

### Body parameters
| Field | Description |
|---|---|
| `input_file_id` | File uploaded with `purpose="batch"`. Each JSONL line is a request: `{ "custom_id": "req-1", "method": "POST", "url": "/v1/chat/completions", "body": { ... } }`. |
| `endpoint` | `/v1/chat/completions`, `/v1/embeddings`, `/v1/completions`, or `/v1/responses`. |
| `completion_window` | Currently only `24h`. |
| `metadata` | Up to 16 string KV pairs. |

When complete the batch object exposes `output_file_id` / `error_file_id`, which you download with **Files → Retrieve file content**.

[Docs](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/batch/create)

Get status: `validating`, `failed`, `in_progress`, `finalizing`, `completed`, `expired`, `cancelling`, `cancelled`.

[Docs](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/batch/retrieve)

List your batches, newest first.

[Docs](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/batch/list)

Best-effort cancel. Already-completed lines remain in the output file.

[Docs](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/batch/cancel)
